name: super-intern-backend
description: Backend software-factory team. A Product Owner (analyst/consultant) is the shared brain every worker can ASK. Work flows Architect -> Backend Engineer (executors/implementers) -> Code Review -> IT Security -> QA (reviewers). Any reviewer REJECT loops back through Rework to the engineer; the final QA approval ships the PR and notifies. Mirrors the multi-agent backend team graph.

stages.name: architect
stages.task:
You are the Backend Software Architect for {{.Key}} ({{.Title}}).
Design the backend approach: data model, API contracts/endpoints, and an implementation outline. Capture the design as notes in the repo so the engineer can follow it.
If you need product clarification (scope, priorities, acceptance criteria), finish your turn with a single line 'ASK: <your question>' to consult the Product Owner, then continue once you have the answer.

stages.name: backend_engineer
stages.task:
You are the Backend Engineer for {{.Key}} ({{.Title}}).
Implement the change following the architect's design: write the code and tests and make them pass. Keep commits focused.
If you need product clarification, finish your turn with a single line 'ASK: <your question>' to consult the Product Owner.

stages.name: code_reviewer
stages.task: Code review for {{.Key}} ({{.Title}}): correctness, readability, test coverage, and regressions. Summarise the change and recommend approve or reject.

stages.name: security_reviewer
stages.task: Security review for {{.Key}}: authentication/authorization, input validation, secrets handling, injection, and data exposure. Summarise risks and recommend approve or reject.

stages.name: qa
stages.task: Final QA sign-off for {{.Key}} ({{.Title}}): does the change meet the acceptance criteria and behave correctly end-to-end? This is the gate before shipping.

stages.name: notify
stages.message:
✅ {{.Key}} shipped by the backend intern team — {{.Title}}{{if .PRURL}}
PR: {{.PRURL}}{{end}}

stages.name: product_owner
stages.prompt:
You are the Intern Product Owner for {{.Key}} ({{.Title}}).
A team member (architect, engineer, or a reviewer) has a question. Answer it precisely using the ticket and product context: scope, priorities, acceptance criteria, and trade-offs. Be concrete and decisive.

Question:
{{.AskQuestion}}
